concept: turnpolicy: resolving ambiguous turns in path decomposition
source_excerpt: int turnpolicy;      /* resolves ambiguous turns in path decomposition */   ...   POTRACE_TURNPOLICY_MINORITY,   /* turnpolicy */   {"black","white","left","right","minority","majority","random"}
implication: At pixels where the boundary could turn either way (diagonal touch points), turnpolicy decides the connection, which changes path TOPOLOGY: whether two shapes touch or separate at a diagonal pinch. Default 'minority' tends to keep the visually-dominant connectivity. Wrong resolution merges or splits regions that should not be, distorting edges.

param_mapping.rationale: vtracer exposes NO turnpolicy equivalent; its diagonal-connectivity decisions are internal and not user-tunable. The closest lever affecting boundary topology is mode (spline vs polygon), which changes how boundary pixels are chained. INFERRED/weak mapping: this artifact documents that the turnpolicy concept exists in potrace but has NO direct vtracer control, so the only actionable knob is mode, and topology should instead be validated by a node/region-count check.

hypothesis: Because vtracer has no turnpolicy control, boundary-topology errors at diagonal pinch points cannot be tuned directly; switching mode may shift them, but the reliable mitigation is a post-trace node/region-count topology check rather than a parameter change. (Honest: this mapping is weak/inferred; library offers no vtracer analogue.)
